components.instruction:
You are given a piece of news text. Your task is to classify this text into one of the following four categories: Business, Sci/Tech, Sports, or World. Base your classification on the primary subject or focus of the news text. 

Pay special attention to news articles that mention companies or industries related to science and technology. For example, if the news is about a business activity (such as revenue forecasts, stock changes, or financial results) of a technology company or a business whose operations are primarily tech-based (such as software companies), you should classify the news as "Sci/Tech" rather than "Business."

Reserve "Business" for stories primarily focused on traditional business activities, markets, finance, or the economy that do not center around advances or events specific to science or technology fields.

When making your classification, consider:
- "Business": General business news, financial reports, stock market updates (not specifically tech-focused), mergers/acquisitions in non-tech sectors, macroeconomic news.
- "Sci/Tech": News involving scientific research, new technology, software/hardware companies, innovations, or breakthroughs. Also includes business operations of tech companies.
- "Sports": News primarily focused on athletic events, teams, players, competitions, or sports industry developments.
- "World": News about international relations, global politics, conflicts, disasters, or major world events (not falling into the other categories).

Output only the single most appropriate category label: Business, Sci/Tech, Sports, or World.
